Monday.focus: Welcome, belonging, and how our classroom works

blocks.Breakfast / Morning Work: Entering the room, unpacking, breakfast cleanup, and starting morning work.
blocks.MOWR / Morning Meeting: Introductions, carpet expectations, active listening, turn-and-talk, and classroom community.
blocks.Heggerty / Phonics / Vocabulary: Practice attention signals, responding together, handling materials, and returning supplies.
blocks.Reading: Read aloud and discuss classroom belonging; practice listening stamina and partner talk.
blocks.Writing: Pencil procedures, notebook setup, name and date, and drawing/writing about hopes for second grade.
blocks.Math: Practice whiteboards, manipulatives, partner talk, cleanup, and explaining thinking with a simple review game.
blocks.Science: Science safety, observation language, asking questions, and handling materials carefully.
blocks.Social Studies: What makes a classroom community? Introduce classroom rules and the Class Promise.
blocks.Pack-up / Dismissal: Backpack procedures, dismissal groups, waiting safely, and leaving calmly.

blocks.Breakfast / Morning Work: Independent start, asking for help, finished-work choices, and voice levels.
blocks.MOWR / Morning Meeting: Review class rules, model expected and unexpected behaviors, and practice attention signals.
blocks.Heggerty / Phonics / Vocabulary: Whole-group response routines, hand signals, turn-taking, and quick transitions.
blocks.Reading: Book care, choosing a reading spot, partner-reading expectations, and building stamina.
blocks.Writing: Using supplies responsibly, complete sentences, sharing materials, and peer-listening expectations.
blocks.Math: Math partner roles, using manipulatives, productive struggle, and asking for clarification.
blocks.Science: Safe versus unsafe choices during investigations; practice cleanup roles.
blocks.Social Studies: Rights and responsibilities in our classroom community.
blocks.Pack-up / Dismissal: Practice pack-up using a visual checklist and dismissal voice levels.

blocks.Breakfast / Morning Work: Practice a timed but calm arrival routine and transitions to morning meeting.
blocks.MOWR / Morning Meeting: Team-building activity, respectful disagreement, and solving small problems peacefully.
blocks.Heggerty / Phonics / Vocabulary: Transition practice between carpet, seats, partners, and independent work.
blocks.Reading: Partner A/Partner B roles, sharing ideas, and responding to a text read aloud.
blocks.Writing: Writing-stamina practice and how to request, borrow, and return classroom supplies.
blocks.Math: Cooperative math challenge emphasizing partner roles, encouragement, and cleanup.
blocks.Science: Observation challenge using classroom objects; practice recording and sharing observations.
blocks.Social Studies: Classroom jobs, leadership, and how each job helps the community.
blocks.Pack-up / Dismissal: Practice emergency changes, early dismissal, and listening for names.

blocks.Breakfast / Morning Work: Complete the arrival routine with reduced teacher prompting.
blocks.MOWR / Morning Meeting: Review logical consequences, reflection, making repairs, and trying again.
blocks.Heggerty / Phonics / Vocabulary: Independent material routines and what to do when confused.
blocks.Reading: Independent reading stamina, book-return procedures, and quiet help signals.
blocks.Writing: Complete a short independent writing task and practice sharing work respectfully.
blocks.Math: Independent task routine, checking work, early-finisher choices, and math cleanup.
blocks.Science: Follow a short investigation routine from materials pickup through cleanup.
blocks.Social Studies: Practice classroom problem scenarios and choose responsible solutions.
blocks.Pack-up / Dismissal: Complete pack-up and dismissal with minimal reminders.

Friday.focus: Practice the full schedule and celebrate readiness

blocks.Breakfast / Morning Work: Run the full arrival routine independently.
blocks.MOWR / Morning Meeting: Review the Class Promise, compliment circle, and celebrate progress.
blocks.Heggerty / Phonics / Vocabulary: Practice the full literacy transition sequence without formal curriculum.
blocks.Reading: Read aloud, partner discussion, and independent reading-stamina celebration.
blocks.Writing: Reflect: What I know about being a successful second grader.
blocks.Math: Review routines through a cooperative math game; no Eureka Math² lesson.
blocks.Science: Review safety and observation routines; no science workbook lesson.
blocks.Social Studies: Classroom community reflection and goal setting; no formal social studies lesson.
blocks.Pack-up / Dismissal: Final full-schedule practice and celebration of classroom readiness.
